description: Google-hosted auth with email/password, phone, and social providers. Tight integration with other Firebase services.

name: Supabase Auth
description: Open-source, Postgres-based auth with row-level security. Free tier up to 50,000 MAUs. Part of the broader Supabase platform.

name: Keycloak
description: Open-source identity and access management by Red Hat. Full control over auth infrastructure — SSO, LDAP federation, social login.

name: Ory (Kratos/Hydra)
description: Open-source identity suite — Kratos (identity), Hydra (OAuth2/OIDC), Keto (permissions), Oathkeeper (access proxy). Most comprehensive open-source auth stack.

name: Lucia
description: Lightweight session-based auth library. **Deprecated as of March 2025** — now a learning resource. Maintainers recommend Oslo and Arctic libraries instead.

name: jose
description: Universal JavaScript JOSE implementation (JWT, JWS, JWE, JWK). Works in Node.js, Deno, Bun, browsers, and edge runtimes. Zero dependencies.

name: jsonwebtoken
description: Classic Node.js JWT implementation. Widely used, simple API for sign/verify.

name: jwt-cli
description: Command-line tool for decoding, encoding, and inspecting JWTs. Useful for debugging tokens during development.

name: PyJWT
description: Python JWT library for encoding and decoding JSON Web Tokens.

name: express-session
description: Server-side session middleware for Express. Supports multiple stores (Redis, Postgres, MongoDB).

name: connect-redis
description: Redis session store for express-session. Production-grade session persistence.

name: iron-session
description: Encrypted/signed cookie-based sessions for Next.js and Express. No database required.

name: CASL
description: Isomorphic JavaScript authorization library. Scales from simple claim-based to full attribute-based access control (ABAC). Integrates with React, Vue, Angular, Prisma.

name: Casbin
description: Language-agnostic authorization library supporting ACL, RBAC, ABAC. Available for Go, Node.js, Python, Java, Rust, and more. Policy defined in configuration files.

name: Oso
description: Authorization framework with a declarative policy language (Polar). Available for Rust, Python, Node.js, Go, Java.

name: Permit.io
description: Authorization-as-a-service platform built on open-source tools (OPA, OPAL). Provides a UI for managing RBAC/ABAC policies.

name: Kong Gateway
description: Open-source API gateway supporting REST, GraphQL, gRPC. Plugin ecosystem for auth, rate limiting, logging, transformations. Industry standard.

name: Traefik
description: Cloud-native application proxy with automatic service discovery. Supports Docker, Kubernetes, and multiple providers. Current version: v3.6.

name: NGINX
description: High-performance web server and reverse proxy. Foundational infrastructure for load balancing and API routing.

name: Caddy
description: Modern web server with automatic HTTPS. Simpler config than NGINX, built-in reverse proxy and load balancing.

name: Apollo Server
description: Production-ready GraphQL server. Part of the Apollo ecosystem (Apollo Client, Apollo Router, GraphOS). Most widely adopted.

name: GraphQL Yoga
description: Lightweight, spec-compliant GraphQL server by The Guild. Works with any schema builder (Pothos, Nexus, vanilla graphql-js).

name: Pothos
description: Plugin-based GraphQL schema builder for TypeScript. Code-first approach with excellent type inference. v4 current.

name: Relay
description: Facebook's GraphQL client framework. Optimized for performance with compile-time query validation and automatic pagination.

name: tRPC
description: End-to-end typesafe APIs for TypeScript. No schemas or code generation needed — types flow from server to client automatically. v11 current.

name: Buf CLI
description: Modern protobuf toolchain replacing `protoc`. Linting, breaking change detection, and code generation for Protocol Buffers.

name: Connect RPC
description: Modern gRPC-compatible RPC framework by Buf. Generates idiomatic TypeScript clients, replaces grpc-web.

name: gRPC-web
description: gRPC for browser clients. Use Connect RPC for new projects; gRPC-web for legacy compatibility.

name: grpcurl
description: Command-line tool for interacting with gRPC servers. Like curl but for gRPC — supports reflection, JSON input.

name: ws
description: Fastest, most popular WebSocket implementation for Node.js. Fully RFC 6455 compliant. ~45M weekly downloads.

name: Socket.IO
description: Real-time bidirectional event-based communication. Automatic reconnection, room support, binary streaming. Falls back to HTTP long-polling. v4.8 current, ~8M weekly downloads.

name: Soketi
description: Open-source WebSocket server built on uWebSockets.js. Pusher-compatible protocol — drop-in replacement for Pusher.

name: Ably
description: Managed real-time messaging platform. Pub/sub, presence, history, push notifications. Free tier available.

name: Pusher
description: Managed real-time infrastructure. Channels for pub/sub, presence channels for user awareness. Widely used.

name: wscat
description: Interactive WebSocket client for the command line. Connect, send messages, inspect responses. Node.js-based.

name: websocat
description: Powerful CLI WebSocket client written in Rust. Unix-pipe-style composition for scripting and automation.

name: Artillery
description: Load testing platform supporting HTTP, WebSocket, Socket.IO, and GraphQL. Serverless and distributed execution.

name: eventsource
description: W3C-compatible EventSource client/polyfill for Node.js and browsers. ~500K weekly downloads.

name: express-sse
description: Simple SSE middleware for Express. Handles connection management and keep-alive.

name: sse-channel
description: SSE broadcasting channel for Node.js. Supports multiple clients, history replay, and auto-reconnect.

name: curl
description: Built-in tool for testing SSE endpoints — `curl -N` streams events directly in the terminal.

name: Helmet.js
description: Sets 15 HTTP security headers with a single middleware call for Express apps. Covers X-Frame-Options, CSP, HSTS, and more.

name: helmet-csp
description: Standalone Content Security Policy middleware extracted from Helmet. Fine-grained CSP directive configuration.

name: cors
description: CORS middleware for Express. Configure allowed origins, methods, headers, and credentials.

name: CSP Evaluator
description: Google's tool for evaluating Content Security Policy effectiveness. Identifies bypasses and weaknesses.

name: Mozilla Observatory
description: Scans website HTTP headers for security problems — CSP, HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, and more. Provides actionable grades.

name: shcheck
description: Python-based security header checker. Scans response headers and reports missing/misconfigured headers.

name: ahead
description: CLI equivalent to securityheaders.io. Scans and grades security headers from the command line.

name: securityheaders
description: Python tool for checking websites for insecure security headers. Supports batch scanning and Docker deployment.

name: express-rate-limit
description: Basic rate-limiting middleware for Express. In-memory by default, supports Redis and other stores.

name: rate-limiter-flexible
description: High-performance rate limiter supporting Redis, Postgres, MongoDB, MySQL, Memcached, and in-memory. Averages 0.7ms per request in cluster mode.

name: rate-limit-redis
description: Redis store for express-rate-limit. Also supports Redict and Valkey.

name: Bottleneck
description: Lightweight, zero-dependency task scheduler and rate limiter for Node.js. Controls concurrency and execution rate.

name: Zod
description: TypeScript-first schema validation with static type inference. The dominant choice for TypeScript projects. Integrates with tRPC, React Hook Form, and most TypeScript frameworks.

name: AJV
description: Fastest JSON Schema validator. Compiles schemas to optimized JavaScript. Supports JSON Schema draft-06/07/2019-09/2020-12.

name: Joi
description: Expressive schema description language and data validator for JavaScript. Rich API with excellent documentation. Server-side focused.

name: Yup
description: Lightweight validation library inspired by Joi. Popular for client-side form validation with Formik and React Hook Form.

name: Pydantic
description: Python data validation using type annotations. v2 has dramatically improved performance. The standard for FastAPI.

name: Marshmallow
description: Python library for object serialization/deserialization and validation. v4.1 current. Flask-Marshmallow for Flask integration.

name: Pact
description: Consumer-driven contract testing. The consumer writes tests defining expectations; the provider verifies against them. Gold standard for microservice contract testing.

name: Dredd
description: Language-agnostic API testing tool that validates implementations against OpenAPI/API Blueprint specs. Sends real HTTP requests.

name: Schemathesis
description: Property-based API testing that generates thousands of edge-case requests from OpenAPI or GraphQL schemas. Built on Hypothesis. v4.13 current.

name: Spectral
description: OpenAPI and AsyncAPI linter. Validates API specs against configurable rulesets. Catches design issues before implementation.

name: ioredis
description: Full-featured Redis client for Node.js. Supports Cluster, Sentinel, Streams, Pipelining, Lua scripting, and Pub/Sub. Used at Alibaba scale.

name: node-redis
description: Official Redis client for Node.js. Supports Redis Stack and Redis 8 features (search, JSON, time-series). Recommended for new projects.

name: redis-cli
description: Command-line interface for Redis. Essential for inspecting cache state, debugging, and manual operations.

name: Valkey
description: Linux Foundation fork of Redis (post-license change). Drop-in Redis replacement. Production-ready v8.1 as of March 2026. Used by AWS ElastiCache.

name: memcached
description: High-performance distributed memory caching system. Lower memory overhead than Redis for simple key-value caching at extreme scale.

name: Sentry
description: Error tracking and performance monitoring with structured logging (added late 2025). SDK v9.17+ for JS, v2.27+ for Python. Source maps, breadcrumbs, release tracking.

name: Bugsnag
description: Error monitoring with strong mobile crash reporting. Deep Android/iOS data including ANR and OOM crashes.

name: SigNoz
description: Open-source alternative to Sentry/Datadog. OpenTelemetry-native, combines metrics, traces, and logs in one platform.

name: Pino
description: Fastest Node.js JSON logger. Minimal overhead, structured output, transport-based architecture. Recommended when performance is critical and bundle size matters.

name: Winston
description: Most popular Node.js logging library. Multiple transports (console, file, HTTP, syslog), flexible formatting. Recommended when transport variety matters.

name: structlog
description: Python structured logging library. Processes log entries through a pipeline of processors. Integrates with Sentry via structlog-sentry.

name: pino-pretty
description: Pretty-prints Pino JSON logs for development. Colorized, human-readable output from structured logs.

name: Vite + React
description: Framework-agnostic build tool and dev server. Scaffolds React/Vue/Svelte/Lit projects via `create-vite`. Supports React, React + SWC, and TypeScript variants out of the box.

name: Next.js
description: Full-stack React framework with SSR, SSG, API routes, and App Router. `create-next-app` scaffolds projects with TypeScript, ESLint, and Tailwind options.

name: SvelteKit
description: Full-stack Svelte framework with SSR, file-based routing, and form actions. Ships 50-70% less JavaScript than React-based frameworks.

name: Astro
description: Content-focused framework with zero-JS-by-default architecture. Supports React, Vue, Svelte, and Solid components via islands architecture.

name: Nuxt
description: Full-stack Vue framework with SSR, SSG, ISR, file-based routing, and the Nitro server engine. Rich module ecosystem.

name: React Router v7
description: Full-stack React framework that merged Remix upstream into React Router. Provides SSR, code splitting, file-based routing, and form handling in "framework mode."

name: Angular CLI
description: Full-featured framework with CLI that handles scaffolding, code generation, building, testing, and deployment. Angular 21 is the current stable release.

name: ESLint
description: The standard JavaScript/TypeScript linter. Flat config is the default since v9. Now supports CSS and HTML linting natively (February 2025). Multithreaded linting in v10.

name: Biome
description: Rust-based all-in-one linter and formatter (97% Prettier-compatible). Single binary, zero dependencies. 423+ lint rules as of v2.3. Replaces ESLint + Prettier with one tool and one config file.

name: Oxlint
description: Rust-based linter with 650+ rules, 50-100x faster than ESLint. Zero-config by default with sensible rule presets for React, Vue, Svelte, and Astro.

name: Prettier
description: Opinionated code formatter for JS/TS/CSS/HTML/JSON/YAML/Markdown. v3 is the current stable release.

name: TypeScript Compiler
description: Type checks TypeScript code. `tsc --noEmit` validates types without producing output, making it ideal as a verification step.

name: typescript-eslint
description: Enables ESLint to lint TypeScript with type information. Provides `strict-type-checked` and `stylistic-type-checked` shared configs.

name: Strict tsconfig Recommendations
description: Configuration presets that maximize TypeScript strictness beyond the default `strict` flag.

name: Stylelint
description: CSS linter with 100+ rules. Supports CSS, SCSS, Less, and CSS-in-JS. The CSS equivalent of ESLint.

name: stylelint-declaration-strict-value
description: Stylelint plugin that enforces CSS custom properties (design tokens) instead of hardcoded values for specified properties. Catches literal hex colors, pixel values, etc. where tokens should be used.

name: Tailwind CSS IntelliSense / tailwind-lint
description: Validates Tailwind class usage, detects conflicting utilities, and enforces correct directive usage. The IntelliSense extension is VS Code-only; `tailwind-lint` brings the same checks to the CLI.

name: eslint-plugin-tailwindcss
description: ESLint plugin with 5+ rules for Tailwind: class ordering, no contradicting classes, no custom classnames (enforce utility-first), and enforced shorthand.

name: Lightning CSS
description: Extremely fast Rust-based CSS parser, transformer, bundler, and minifier. Replaces PostCSS + autoprefixer + postcss-preset-env in one tool.

name: PostCSS
description: CSS transformation framework with a plugin ecosystem. Handles autoprefixing, nesting, custom properties polyfills, and more.

name: Vite
description: Dev server with native ES module serving and Rollup-based production bundling. HMR is near-instant regardless of app size.

name: Turbopack
description: Rust-based bundler from Vercel, now the default in Next.js 16. Claims 700x faster updates than webpack and 10x faster than Vite for large apps. Cold starts under 1 second for 30,000+ module apps.

name: Rspack
description: Drop-in webpack replacement written in Rust. 10-23x faster than webpack 5 with ~85% webpack ecosystem compatibility (loaders, plugins).

name: esbuild
description: Extremely fast Go-based bundler and minifier. Cold production builds in ~300ms for small libraries, ~700ms for larger ones. 10-100x faster than webpack.

name: webpack
description: The original JavaScript module bundler. Massive plugin ecosystem. Still powers many production applications.

name: shadcn/ui
description: Copy-paste component collection built on Radix UI (or Base UI as of Feb 2026) and Tailwind CSS. Components live in your codebase, not node_modules. 75,000+ GitHub stars.

name: Radix UI Primitives
description: Unstyled, accessible React component primitives. Handles ARIA, keyboard navigation, and focus management. The foundation under shadcn/ui.

name: Headless UI
description: Unstyled, accessible UI components from the Tailwind CSS team. Covers common patterns: dialogs, menus, listboxes, comboboxes, tabs, transitions.

name: Mantine
description: Batteries-included React component library with 120+ components and 100+ hooks. Covers rich text editors, date pickers, notifications, spotlight search, and drag-and-drop.

name: Material UI
description: React implementation of Google's Material Design. 90+ components, extensive theming system, enterprise support.

name: Chakra UI
description: Accessible React component library with style props API. Strong focus on accessibility (WCAG compliance).

name: Stylelint + stylelint-declaration-strict-value
description: Enforces that specified CSS properties use variables/tokens instead of hardcoded values. Catches literal hex colors, pixel values, and font names where design tokens should be used.

name: ESLint Custom Rules
description: Custom ESLint rules that catch hardcoded color values, spacing, and non-token style usage in JSX/TSX files.

name: Semgrep
description: Static analysis tool that can write pattern-based rules for any language, including CSS and HTML. Can enforce design system token usage and catch non-compliant patterns.

name: Tokens Studio
description: Syncs design tokens from Figma to code via GitHub/GitLab. Supports color, typography, spacing, border-radius, and shadow tokens. 264,000+ active users.

name: Lighthouse CI
description: Runs Lighthouse audits (performance, accessibility, best practices, SEO) in CI or locally. Sets performance budgets and fails builds when Core Web Vitals (LCP, CLS, TBT) regress.

name: web-vitals
description: Tiny library (1.5KB) for measuring Core Web Vitals (LCP, CLS, INP, FCP, TTFB) in the browser. Reports real user metrics.

name: size-limit
description: Calculates the real cost (size + load time) of your JS. Modular architecture with plugins for webpack, esbuild, and time estimation. Shows pull request size impact via GitHub Action.

name: webpack-bundle-analyzer
description: Generates interactive treemap visualizations of webpack bundle contents. Shows parsed, gzipped, and Brotli sizes of every module.

name: bundlesize
description: Tests asset sizes against thresholds. Integrates with GitHub to post pass/fail status on PRs. Supports gzip and Brotli compression.

name: Vitest
description: Vite-native testing framework with Jest-compatible API. Smart watch mode, native v8/istanbul coverage, browser mode for component testing. Supports Vue, React, Svelte, Lit, and Marko.

name: Playwright MCP Server
description: Official MCP server that gives Claude Code direct browser control. 34 tools across core automation, tab management, vision-based interactions, and test assertions. Uses accessibility snapshots by default (no screenshots needed for structure).

name: Playwright DevTools MCP
description: Specialized MCP server providing Chrome DevTools Protocol access through Playwright. Enables autonomous debugging, performance analysis, and security inspection via DevTools.

name: Playwright Test Runner
description: End-to-end testing framework with cross-browser support (Chromium, Firefox, WebKit). Auto-waiting, trace recording, and parallel test execution.

name: Figma Dev Mode MCP
description: Exposes Figma design structure (hierarchy, auto-layout, variants, text styles, spacing tokens, component references) directly to Claude Code.

name: visual-regression-tester
description: `jeremylongshore/claude-code-plugins-plus-skills` marketplace

name: claude-code-frontend-dev
description: [GitHub](https://github.com/hemangjoshi37a/claude-code-frontend-dev)

name: AccessLint
description: `accesslint/claude-marketplace`

name: accessibility-agents
description: [GitHub](https://github.com/Community-Access/accessibility-agents)

name: claude-a11y-skill
description: [GitHub](https://github.com/airowe/claude-a11y-skill)

name: skill-creator
description: Official marketplace

name: commit-commands
description: Official marketplace

name: @axe-core/playwright
description: Accessibility testing integrated with Playwright

name: pa11y
description: Automated accessibility testing

name: pa11y-ci
description: CI-friendly accessibility testing

name: Stylelint
description: CSS linting and design system enforcement

name: htmlhint
description: HTML linting

name: dotnet format
description: Built-in SDK formatter and linter (since .NET 6). Enforces `.editorconfig` rules for whitespace, code style, and analyzer diagnostics. Three subcommands: `whitespace`, `style`, `analyzers`.

name: EditorConfig
description: Configuration file (`.editorconfig`) that defines code style rules consumed by `dotnet format`, Roslyn analyzers, and IDEs. Supports .NET-specific rules like `dotnet_style_*` and `csharp_style_*`.

name: StyleCop Analyzers
description: Roslyn-based analyzers enforcing StyleCop's layout, ordering, spacing, readability, and documentation rules. ~200 rules with SA-prefix diagnostic IDs.

name: Roslynator
description: 500+ analyzers, refactorings, and code fixes for C#, powered by Roslyn. Actively maintained under the `dotnet` GitHub org. Analyzers package contains 200+ diagnostic rules; refactorings package adds 200+ more.

name: Meziantou.Analyzer
description: Roslyn analyzer enforcing best practices for performance, security, usage, and design in C#. Actively maintained with frequent releases.

name: CSharpier
description: Opinionated code formatter for C# (inspired by Prettier). Parses and re-prints code using its own rules, eliminating style debates. Minimal configuration by design. v1.2.6 is current.

name: xUnit
description: The most widely adopted .NET test framework. Default in Visual Studio templates, 469M+ NuGet downloads, used by 65%+ of C# open-source projects. Supports parallel execution, theory/data-driven tests, and extensible assertions.

name: NUnit
description: Mature framework with rich attribute-driven configuration (`[TestCase]`, `[SetUp]`, `[TearDown]`, custom constraints). Best for legacy codebases and teams needing parameterized test metadata.

name: MSTest
description: Microsoft's built-in test framework, included with Visual Studio. MSTest v3/v4 are modern and competitive. Simplest setup for new developers.

name: FluentAssertions
description: Fluent assertion syntax for readable test expectations. Supports xUnit, NUnit, MSTest, MSpec, and TUnit. Rich failure messages with expected/actual diffs.

name: Shouldly
description: Terse assertion library focused on great error messages. Extension-method syntax: `result.ShouldBe(42)`. v4.3.0 targets .NET 8+ and .NET Standard 2.0.

name: NSubstitute
description: Friendly mocking library with clean syntax. `Substitute.For<IService>()` creates mocks with minimal ceremony. No `Setup`/`Verify` boilerplate.

name: Moq
description: Strongly-typed mocking with `Setup`/`Verify` pattern. More control over mock behavior at the cost of verbosity. Widely used in enterprise codebases.

name: FakeItEasy
description: Simple mocking library with a focus on ease of use. `A.Fake<IService>()` creates fakes. Syntax falls between NSubstitute's brevity and Moq's explicitness.

name: Bogus
description: Fake data generator for .NET. Generates realistic names, emails, addresses, dates, and domain-specific data using a fluent API. Inspired by faker.js.

name: AutoFixture
description: Automatic test object creation. Generates anonymous instances of any class, filling properties with random but valid data. Integrates with xUnit, NUnit, NSubstitute, Moq.

name: Verify
description: Snapshot testing for .NET. Serializes test output to a `.verified.txt` file and diffs against it on subsequent runs. Supports xUnit, NUnit, MSTest, and Expecto.

name: Stryker.NET
description: Mutation testing for .NET. Automatically mutates source code (flips conditionals, removes statements, changes operators) and verifies tests catch the mutations. Generates a mutation score report.

name: .NET Analyzers (CA rules)
description: Built-in Roslyn analyzers included with the .NET SDK. CA-prefix rules cover security, performance, design, globalization, and reliability. IDE-prefix rules cover code style.

name: SonarAnalyzer.CSharp
description: 380+ C# rules and 130+ VB.NET rules from SonarSource. Covers bugs, vulnerabilities, code smells, and security hotspots. Works standalone (NuGet) or with SonarQube/SonarCloud.

name: Semgrep
description: Lightweight static analysis supporting 30+ languages including C# (GA). Pattern-based rules that look like source code. Semgrep Supply Chain scans NuGet dependencies for reachable vulnerabilities.

name: SecurityCodeScan
description: Vulnerability pattern detector for C# and VB.NET. Detects SQL injection, XSS, CSRF, XXE, and other OWASP Top 10 issues.

name: NDepend
description: Enterprise static analysis with deep interprocedural flow analysis, cross-project dependency mapping, architectural rule enforcement, and technical debt tracking. 150+ default code rules.

name: dotnet CLI
description: Unified CLI for building, testing, publishing, packaging, and managing .NET projects. Core commands: `build`, `test`, `run`, `publish`, `pack`, `tool`, `new`.

name: MSBuild
description: The build engine behind `dotnet build`. Direct `msbuild` commands give finer control: custom targets, property evaluation (`-getProperty`), binary logging (`-bl`).

name: Directory.Build.props
description: MSBuild file placed at the repo root that applies common properties to all projects in the solution. Centralizes `<LangVersion>`, `<Nullable>`, `<TreatWarningsAsErrors>`, analyzer packages, etc.

name: Directory.Build.targets
description: Like `Directory.Build.props` but evaluated after individual project files. Used for custom build targets, post-build steps, and conditional logic that depends on per-project properties.

name: Central Package Management
description: Centralizes NuGet package versions in a single `Directory.Packages.props` file. Projects use `<PackageReference>` without `Version`; versions are resolved from the central file.

name: global.json
description: Pins the .NET SDK version for the repo. Ensures consistent builds across developer machines and CI.

name: Roslyn Source Generators
description: Compile-time code generation with full access to the Roslyn semantic model. Runs as part of every build, zero runtime overhead, full IDE support. The `IIncrementalGenerator` API (Roslyn 4.0+/.NET 6+) is the modern standard.

name: T4 Templates
description: Text template engine for generating any text output (C#, SQL, config, HTML). Available since .NET 3.5. Runs outside the build pipeline by default, though [T4.SourceGenerator](https://github.com/CptWesley/T4.SourceGenerator) bridges to Roslyn.

name: dotnet new (templates)
description: Project and item scaffolding. Built-in templates for webapi, console, classlib, blazor, etc. Custom templates via `template.json`.

name: EF Core Scaffolding
description: Reverse-engineers a database into C# entity classes and a DbContext. Supports T4 template customization for output format.

name: dotnet add package
description: Adds a NuGet package reference to a project file. Resolves the latest stable version by default, or a specified version.

name: dotnet list package
description: Lists all package references for a project or solution. Flags: `--vulnerable`, `--deprecated`, `--outdated`, `--include-transitive`.

name: NuGet Audit
description: Automatic vulnerability scanning during `dotnet restore`. NuGet Audit 2.0 scans direct and transitive dependencies against the GitHub Advisory Database. In .NET 10+, audit mode defaults to "all" (direct + transitive).

name: NuGet CLI (nuget.exe)
description: Standalone NuGet command-line tool for restore, pack, push, and config management. Required for some .NET Framework scenarios.

name: XML Documentation Comments
description: Triple-slash (`///`) comments with XML tags (`<summary>`, `<param>`, `<returns>`, `<exception>`, `<example>`). Compiler generates `.xml` files consumed by IntelliSense and doc generators.

name: DocFX
description: Microsoft's static site generator for .NET API documentation. Consumes XML doc comments and Markdown to produce a searchable HTML site. Supports C#, VB.NET, and F#. The successor to Sandcastle.

name: Sandcastle Help File Builder
description: Legacy documentation generator producing CHM, MSHV, and website output from XML comments. Command-line based, no GUI.

name: dotnet list package --vulnerable
description: Lists packages with known security vulnerabilities from the GitHub Advisory Database. Add `--include-transitive` to scan the full dependency graph.

name: dotnet list package --deprecated
description: Lists packages marked as deprecated by their authors, including suggested replacements.

name: Snyk .NET
description: Commercial SCA platform with .NET/NuGet support. Scans for vulnerabilities, provides remediation advice, and monitors continuously. Integrates with CI/CD via GitHub Actions (`snyk/actions/dotnet`).

name: OWASP Dependency-Check
description: Open-source SCA tool scanning project dependencies against the NVD (National Vulnerability Database). Supports .NET via CLI or plugins.

name: BenchmarkDotNet
description: The standard .NET microbenchmarking library. Automates warmup, iteration, and statistical analysis. Produces markdown/HTML reports with execution time, memory allocation, and GC metrics. Used by Microsoft and .NET OSS maintainers.

name: dotnet-counters
description: Live performance monitoring for running .NET processes. Displays EventCounter and Meter API values (CPU, GC, threadpool, exceptions, custom counters) in real time.

name: dotnet-trace
description: Collects detailed runtime traces from a running .NET process without a native debugger. Exports to `.nettrace` format (convertible to `.speedscope.json` or Chromium trace).

name: dotnet-dump
description: Collects and analyzes process dumps without a native debugger. Supports SOS debugging commands (`dumpheap`, `dumpobj`, `gcroot`) for memory leak investigation.

name: dotnet-gcdump
description: Collects GC (garbage collector) dumps showing live object graphs. Lighter than a full process dump -- captures only managed heap information.

name: PerfView
description: Microsoft's free advanced performance analysis tool for Windows. Analyzes ETW traces, GC behavior, JIT compilation, CPU sampling, and memory allocation. Steep learning curve but unmatched depth.

name: dotnet-monitor
description: Sidecar diagnostic tool that exposes REST APIs for collecting traces, dumps, counters, and logs from running .NET apps. Designed for containerized/production environments.

name: Verify (VerifyTests)
description: Snapshot testing tool for .NET that simplifies assertion of complex data models, documents, and images. The de facto standard for snapshot testing in .NET.

name: ApprovalTests.Net
description: Approval-based verification library with dedicated WPF and WinForms screenshot extensions.

name: Snapshooter
description: Snapshot testing tool for .NET Core and .NET Framework with JSON-focused comparison.

name: FlaUI
description: .NET UI automation library based on Microsoft's UI Automation (UIA) framework. Supports UIA2 (legacy) and UIA3 (modern, recommended). Works with WPF, WinForms, WinUI 3, and UWP apps. Provides strongly-typed access to UI elements with readable, fluent APIs.

name: FlaUInspect
description: Visual inspection tool for exploring UI Automation trees of running Windows applications. The modern replacement for Inspect.exe.

name: Microsoft UI Automation (UIA)
description: The built-in Windows accessibility and automation framework. Ships with .NET Framework and Windows SDK.

name: WinAppDriver
description: Microsoft's Selenium/Appium-compatible service for automating Windows desktop app UI. Supports UWP, WinUI 3, WPF, WinForms, and Win32. Runs as a local REST service on `http://127.0.0.1:4723/` and accepts WebDriver-protocol commands.

name: NovaWindows Driver (Appium)
description: Open-source Appium 2 driver for Windows desktop automation. Drop-in replacement for the abandoned WinAppDriver/Appium Windows driver.

name: Accessibility Insights for Windows
description: Free, open-source Microsoft tool for finding and fixing accessibility issues in Windows apps. The recommended replacement for legacy SDK accessibility tools.

name: Axe.Windows
description: The programmatic accessibility testing engine that powers Accessibility Insights. Runs automated accessibility scans from code or CLI.

name: Inspect.exe
description: Windows SDK tool for viewing UIA properties and MSAA properties of any UI element.

name: AccChecker (UI Accessibility Checker)
description: Legacy Windows SDK tool for verifying UIA and MSAA implementations. Deprecated — use Accessibility Insights or Axe.Windows instead.

name: UI Automation Verify (UIA Verify)
description: Windows SDK framework for manual and automated testing of UIA implementations.

name: Verify.ImageSharp.Compare
description: Verify extension that adds image comparison using ImageSharp.Compare. Best-in-class for integrating visual regression into .NET test suites.

name: ImageSharp.Compare
description: Cross-platform .NET image comparison library. Calculates mean error, absolute error, and generates diff images.

name: Visual Regression Tracker
description: Self-hosted, open-source platform for managing visual regression test results across projects.

name: Windows UI Kit for Figma
description: Official Microsoft Figma library with WinUI 3 components, design patterns, colors, typography, and spacing tokens.

name: Fluent 2 Design System
description: Microsoft's cross-platform design system with tokens for color, typography, spacing, and elevation.

name: Design System Compliance Checker (Figma Plugin)
description: Figma plugin that audits design files for consistent use of design system styles and components.

name: XAML Styler
description: Visual Studio extension that formats XAML source code based on configurable styling rules.

name: XAML Data Binding Diagnostics (Visual Studio)
description: Built-in Visual Studio tool that displays binding failures in a dedicated tool window.

name: Live Visual Tree / Live Property Explorer (Visual Studio)
description: Built-in Visual Studio debugging tools for inspecting the runtime XAML visual tree.

name: XAML Hot Reload (Visual Studio)
description: Edit XAML while the app is running and see changes applied without restarting.

name: Snoop WPF
description: Open-source WPF spy utility for browsing the visual tree, logical tree, and automation tree of running WPF applications.

name: Windows Performance Recorder (WPR)
description: ETW-based system-wide performance recording tool. Part of the Windows Assessment and Deployment Kit (ADK).

name: Windows Performance Analyzer (WPA)
description: Visual analysis tool for ETW traces captured by WPR. Available standalone from the Microsoft Store.

name: Visual Studio Application Timeline
description: Built-in Visual Studio profiler for XAML app resource consumption. Replaced the older "XAML UI Responsiveness" tool.

name: Appium for .NET MAUI
description: The officially recommended approach for .NET MAUI UI testing. Uses Appium with platform-specific drivers.

name: Xappium.UITest
description: Higher-level abstraction over Appium specifically designed for cross-platform .NET MAUI testing.

name: Plugin.Maui.UITestHelpers
description: Community NuGet package providing MAUI-specific UI test helper methods.

name: dotnet build
description: Compiles the project and its dependencies. For WinUI/WPF/MAUI, the target framework includes a Windows version specifier.

name: dotnet publish
description: Compiles, packages, and prepares the app for deployment. Supports MSIX (packaged), unpackaged, self-contained, trimmed, and AOT modes.

name: dotnet run
description: Builds and runs the project in one step.

name: dotnet msbuild
description: Provides direct access to MSBuild with full property and target control. Useful when `dotnet build` flags are insufficient.

name: Appium Windows Driver
description: Appium's interface to WinAppDriver. Provides the Appium protocol layer on top of WinAppDriver, enabling cross-platform test orchestration. Supports UWP, WPF, WinForms, and Win32 apps on Windows 10+.

name: TestStack.White
description: Legacy .NET UI automation framework based on UIAutomation. Supported Win32, WinForms, WPF, and Silverlight.

name: Snoop
description: Open-source WPF spy/inspector utility. Browses the visual tree, logical tree, and automation tree of any running WPF app without a debugger. Allows live property editing, trigger inspection, and breakpoints on property changes.

name: MVVM Unit Testing
description: ViewModels in WPF/MAUI follow MVVM, making them testable without UI automation. Test commands, property changes (`INotifyPropertyChanged`), and service interactions using standard unit test frameworks.

name: Appium for MAUI
description: Microsoft's recommended approach for MAUI UI testing. Uses the Appium WebDriver protocol with platform-specific drivers (WinAppDriver on Windows, XCUITest on iOS, UIAutomator2 on Android) to automate MAUI apps across all targets.

name: DeviceRunners
description: Runs unit tests directly on physical devices or emulators. Splits the UI from the test runner so tests execute in the context of a running MAUI app. Supports xUnit and NUnit.

name: MAUI Unit Testing
description: Standard .NET unit tests targeting MAUI ViewModels, services, and converters. No MAUI runtime required -- tests run in a plain .NET test project.

name: MakeAppx.exe
description: Creates MSIX/APPX packages and bundles from loose files. Also extracts, encrypts, and decrypts packages. Included in the Windows SDK.

name: SignTool.exe
description: Digitally signs MSIX packages and executables. Required for MSIX installation -- unsigned packages cannot be installed.

name: Windows App Certification Kit
description: Validates that an MSIX package meets Microsoft Store requirements. Tests security, performance, and API compliance. Required before Store submission.

name: Microsoft Store Developer CLI
description: Cross-platform CLI (Windows, macOS, Linux) for publishing apps to the Microsoft Store. Configures projects, creates MSIX packages, and submits to Partner Center APIs.

name: GitHub Actions
description: GitHub-hosted Windows runners include the .NET SDK, Windows SDK, and MSBuild. Builds WinUI, WPF, and MAUI Windows targets. The `windows-latest` image includes Visual Studio Build Tools.

name: Azure DevOps Pipelines
description: Microsoft's CI/CD platform with deep MSIX integration. MSIX Packaging Extension available from the Visual Studio Marketplace. Supports Azure Key Vault for certificate management.

name: WACK GitHub Action
description: GitHub Action that runs the Windows App Certification Kit and generates test results as artifacts.

name: XAML Hot Reload
description: Incrementally updates XAML UI in a running WPF, UWP, WinUI 3, or MAUI app without restarting. Starting in Visual Studio 2022 v17.14 Preview 2, also available at design-time (not just during debugging).

name: Live Visual Tree
description: Built-in Visual Studio tool that displays the live visual tree of a running XAML app (WPF, UWP, WinUI 3, MAUI). Allows inspecting and editing properties in real time during debugging.

name: UWPSpy
description: Inspection tool for UWP and WinUI 3 applications. Views and manipulates UI elements and their properties in real time. Uses the XAML Diagnostic APIs (Windows 10 v1703+).

name: WinUI3.Design.Diagnostics
description: In-app diagnostics tool for WinUI 3 providing a live visual tree, style inspector, and resource lookup tracer. Embeds directly in your app.

name: Roslyn Analyzers & StyleCop
description: Static code analysis integrated into the build. Roslyn analyzers run during `dotnet build` and report diagnostics. StyleCop.Analyzers enforces C# style rules. Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.NetAnalyzers provides CA-prefixed quality rules.

name: dotnet test
description: Runs unit and integration tests for xUnit, NUnit, MSTest, and TUnit projects. Supports two test platforms: VSTest (legacy) and Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP, modern).

name: Coverlet + ReportGenerator
description: Coverlet collects code coverage data during test runs. ReportGenerator converts coverage XML into human-readable HTML reports.

name: C# LSP Plugin
description: Enables IDE-like code intelligence in Claude Code for C# projects. Provides go-to-definition, find-references, hover type information, and real-time diagnostics. Finding all call sites takes ~50ms (vs ~45s with text search).

name: csharp-lsp-mcp
description: MCP server providing C# and XAML language intelligence for Claude Code. Exposes IntelliSense, diagnostics, and code analysis through the Model Context Protocol. Supports both `.cs` and `.xaml` files.

name: Claude Code LSP
description: Non-IDE-dependent LSP integration for Claude Code. Supports 11 languages including C#. Provides go-to-definition, find-references, and diagnostics.
